title: Number of deaths by age
subtitle: The estimated number of deaths in each age group. Estimates may be uncertain for countries with poor [vital registration systems](#dod:crvs).
caption:
Number of deaths by age

The estimated number of deaths in each age group. Estimates may be uncertain for countries with poor [vital registration systems](#dod:crvs).

This horizontal bar chart displays the estimated number of deaths in each age group for the world in 2023, with values ranging from hundreds of thousands to millions. The bars are arranged vertically by age group, starting from 100+ years at the top and descending to 0-4 years at the bottom. The highest number of deaths occurs in the 80-84 years age group, with 6.807 million deaths, followed closely by the 70-74 years group with 6.591 million and the 75-79 years group with 6.578 million. These three age groups form a cluster near the top of the chart, each exceeding 6.5 million deaths. The 65-69 years group also shows a high value at 5.749 million, while the 85-89 years group records 5.958 million deaths. In contrast, younger age groups show significantly lower numbers: the 0-4 years group has 4.825 million deaths, the 5-9 years group has 699,800, and the 10-14 years group has 453,900. The 15-19 years group records 673,000 deaths, and the 20-24 years group has 864,000. The 25-29 years group shows 927,500 deaths, while the 30-34 years group has 1.045 million. The 35-39 years group records 1.224 million, and the 40-44 years group has 1.504 million. The 45-49 years group shows 1.856 million deaths, and the 50-54 years group has 2.628 million. The 55-59 years group records 3.592 million, and the 60-64 years group has 4.483 million. The 90-94 years group shows 3.644 million deaths, while the 95-99 years group has 1.306 million. The 100+ years group records the lowest value at 243,400 deaths. The data shows a clear pattern where death counts rise with age, peak in the 80-84 years group, and then decline for older age groups.
